name: Syria

colors.symbolism: the bloodshed in the struggle against colonialism and for Arab unity
a bright future for the Arab nation
the dark past of oppression and foreign domination
Islam and the Fatimid dynasty; the colour of the two stars

description: Three equal horizontal stripes — red on top, white in the middle, black at the bottom — with two green five-pointed stars side by side, centred on the white middle stripe.
history: Originally adopted in 1958 as the flag of the United Arab Republic (Egypt and Syria); Syria continued to use the design after the UAR's dissolution in 1961, then replaced it with other Arab tricolours, before re-adopting it on 30 March 1980 under the Ba'athist regime. It remained Syria's flag throughout the rule of Hafez al-Assad and his son Bashar al-Assad, until the fall of the Assad government on 8 December 2024, after which the post-Assad transitional government adopted the green-white-black 'independence flag' as Syria's national flag.

features.symbolism: the colours of the Arab Liberation Flag of the 1952 Egyptian revolution — red for the struggle, white for a bright future, black for the dark past of oppression

originally the union of Egypt and Syria (and later, by extension, Iraq) as the United Arab Republic; after the UAR's dissolution the two stars were retained to represent the two then-united Ba'athist republics, Syria and Iraq
